issues: teal blade partially blends with teal-grid background tiles
composite renders as two overlapping sword sprites creating ambiguous double-weapon silhouette
silhouette not immediately readable as single dagger at glance
reasoning: The dagger_quick_v01 composite shows two overlapping pixel-art sword sprites (silver/gray handle + teal/cyan blade) placed at the boundary between the dark-red and dark-gray tile zones. Locatability is acceptable — the contrasting silver handle and teal highlights pop against the dark surroundings, and the asset is spottable within ~0.5s aided by the red arrow marker. However, the cyan/teal blade channel overlaps meaningfully with the teal hue of the dungeon grid tiles, reducing edge contrast on the blade portion. More critically, the composite appears to render two daggers stacked at a slight offset, producing a doubled silhouette that reads as two weapons rather than one clean item — this is a native-vs-pasted integration concern. The pixel art style is broadly consistent with the Echoes aesthetic and there is no severe tonal clash, but the muddy silhouette and partial bg-hue blending keep this in marginal territory.
